criteria_verification.evidence: dispatch_autobuild_async invokes forward_context_builder.build_for(stage=StageClass.AUTOBUILD, build_id=build_id, feature_id=feature_id) as the very first step (before any stage_log write). Test TestForwardContextResolution.test_calls_builder_with_autobuild_stage_and_feature_id installs a spy on builder.build_for and asserts the args are exactly {stage: AUTOBUILD, build_id, feature_id}.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Calls DeepAgents middleware `start_async_task` with the autobuild
criteria_verification.evidence: The dispatcher invokes async_task_starter.start_async_task(subagent_name=AUTOBUILD_RUNNER_NAME, context=launch_payload) where AUTOBUILD_RUNNER_NAME='autobuild_runner' and launch_payload includes build_id, feature_id, correlation_id, and serialised context_entries. TestStartAsyncTaskInvocation asserts the subagent name and the four payload fields.
criteria_verification.notes: The dispatcher invokes async_task_starter.start_async_task(subagent_name=AUTOBUILD_RUNNER_NAME, context=launch_payload) where AUTOBUILD_RUNNER_NAME='autobuild_runner' and launch_payload includes build_id, feature_id, correlation_id, and serialised context_entries. TestStartAsyncTaskInvocation asserts the subagent name and the four payload fields.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Returns the assigned `task_id` immediately; does not await completion
criteria_verification.evidence: dispatch_autobuild_async is a synchronous function that returns AutobuildDispatchHandle(task_id, ...) immediately after start_async_task returns the minted id. There is no await/poll for runner completion. TestSynchronousReturn.test_returns_handle_with_minted_task_id asserts the returned handle carries the starter-minted task_id, and TestSynchronousReturn.test_dispatch_does_not_await_runner_completion uses a non-executing starter to confirm no runner-body hook is invoked.
criteria_verification.notes: dispatch_autobuild_async is a synchronous function that returns AutobuildDispatchHandle(task_id, ...) immediately after start_async_task returns the minted id. There is no await/poll for runner completion. TestSynchronousReturn.test_returns_handle_with_minted_task_id asserts the returned handle carries the starter-minted task_id, and TestSynchronousReturn.test_dispatch_does_not_await_runner_completion uses a non-executing starter to confirm no runner-body hook is invoked.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Initialises the `async_tasks` channel entry with an `AutobuildState`
criteria_verification.evidence: After start_async_task returns, the dispatcher calls state_channel.initialise_autobuild_state(build_id, feature_id, task_id, correlation_id, lifecycle=AUTOBUILD_STARTING_LIFECYCLE='starting', wave_index=0, task_index=0). TestAsyncTasksStateChannelInit.test_state_channel_entry_has_starting_lifecycle asserts every field; the correlation_id assertion is in TestCorrelationIdThreading.test_correlation_id_appears_on_state_channel_entry.
criteria_verification.notes: After start_async_task returns, the dispatcher calls state_channel.initialise_autobuild_state(build_id, feature_id, task_id, correlation_id, lifecycle=AUTOBUILD_STARTING_LIFECYCLE='starting', wave_index=0, task_index=0). TestAsyncTasksStateChannelInit.test_state_channel_entry_has_starting_lifecycle asserts every field; the correlation_id assertion is in TestCorrelationIdThreading.test_correlation_id_appears_on_state_channel_entry.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Records the autobuild dispatch as a `stage_log` entry in
criteria_verification.evidence: stage_log_recorder.record_running is called twice on the happy path: once before start_async_task with details_json {subagent, correlation_id, context_entries, task_id: None} and once after with task_id populated. The recorder is upsert-shaped on (build_id, feature_id, stage), so the live row reflects the post-dispatch row. TestStageLogRecording.test_post_dispatch_stage_log_carries_task_id asserts details_json["task_id"] equals the returned handle.task_id; test_stage_log_recorded_with_running_state_and_autobuild_stage asserts both calls target StageClass.AUTOBUILD.
criteria_verification.notes: stage_log_recorder.record_running is called twice on the happy path: once before start_async_task with details_json {subagent, correlation_id, context_entries, task_id: None} and once after with task_id populated. The recorder is upsert-shaped on (build_id, feature_id, stage), so the live row reflects the post-dispatch row. TestStageLogRecording.test_post_dispatch_stage_log_carries_task_id asserts details_json["task_id"] equals the returned handle.task_id; test_stage_log_recorded_with_running_state_and_autobuild_stage asserts both calls target StageClass.AUTOBUILD.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Two concurrent builds dispatching autobuild at the same time receive
criteria_verification.evidence: TestConcurrentDispatchesGetDistinctTaskIds.test_two_concurrent_dispatches_get_distinct_task_ids spawns two threads dispatching autobuild for build-A/FEAT-A and build-B/FEAT-B against a single FakeAsyncTaskStarter (which uses a threading.Lock-guarded counter). The test asserts len(set(task_ids)) == 2 — i.e. distinct identifiers. The dispatcher itself is stateless across calls, so the only synchronisation requirement is on the AsyncTaskStarter Protocol's contract that start_async_task mints a fresh id per call.
criteria_verification.notes: TestConcurrentDispatchesGetDistinctTaskIds.test_two_concurrent_dispatches_get_distinct_task_ids spawns two threads dispatching autobuild for build-A/FEAT-A and build-B/FEAT-B against a single FakeAsyncTaskStarter (which uses a threading.Lock-guarded counter). The test asserts len(set(task_ids)) == 2 — i.e. distinct identifiers. The dispatcher itself is stateless across calls, so the only synchronisation requirement is on the AsyncTaskStarter Protocol's contract that start_async_task mints a fresh id per call.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Independent verification confirmed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 281 chars provided.
